metadata.title: Research the MGMT Methylation Biomarker: A Predictive Epigenetic Mark That Flips the Intuition
metadata.color_semantics: teardown: INK #2A1A0E body text; CRIMSON #C8102E key finding/vulnerability; SLATE #545454 structure/neutral
metadata.note: CLI video REQUIRED spine: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(manim) · B05 CHANGE · B06 OUTPUT(manim) · B07 SUMMARY · B08 NEXT STEPS · B09 outro. Source: cancer-biology/chapters/07-epigenetics-in-cancer-the-methylation-and-histone-code.md

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. MGMT — a broken gene that helps the drug work.

lines: MGMT — a broken gene that helps the drug work.

beats.narration_text: In glioblastoma, patients whose tumor has a silenced repair gene survive longer on temozolomide than patients whose repair gene is active. A broken repair system is an advantage for the patient. The test for it is a methylation pattern, not a mutation. This counterintuitive result is now standard clinical practice.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- problem card: counterintuitive result; broken repair gene helps drug work

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: ask Claude to research the clinical significance of MGMT promoter methylation and its predictive value.

props.command:
claude "Research MGMT promoter methylation in
glioblastoma. What fraction of GBM patients have
MGMT methylation? What is the survival difference
for methylated vs unmethylated patients on
temozolomide (cite Hegi 2005 NEJM)? Is there
evidence unmethylated patients should receive
alternative therapy? Distinguish predictive from
prognostic value."

beats.role_note: terminal (Onda) -- Python script reading Claude response
beats.narration_text: Read the code. The script builds the predictive versus prognostic distinction table — methylated branch versus unmethylated branch with survival numbers from the Hegi 2005 trial.

props.code:
# mgmt_decision.py -- Claude Code output
BRANCHES = [
  {"status": "Methylated",
   "freq": "40-45% of GBM",
   "tmz_response": "YES -- MGMT silenced",
   "median_os_mo": 21.7,
   "recommendation": "Temozolomide standard"},
  {"status": "Unmethylated",
   "freq": "55-60% of GBM",
   "tmz_response": "MINIMAL -- MGMT active",
   "median_os_mo": 12.7,
   "recommendation": "Clinical trial preferred"},
]
print(f"{'Status':<14} {'TMZ Response':<22} {'OS (mo)':<10} Recommendation")
print("-" * 70)
for b in BRANCHES:
  print(f"{b['status']:<14} {b['tmz_response']:<22} "
        f"{b['median_os_mo']:<10} {b['recommendation']}")

beats.narration_text: The decision tree. GBM patient at top. MGMT methylation test. Left branch: methylated, forty to forty-five percent of GBM patients, plus five point three months overall survival on temozolomide. Right branch: unmethylated, fifty-five to sixty percent, minimal benefit. The counterintuitive reading: the broken branch is the good branch.
beats.visual_intent: Manim decision tree: GBM patient -> MGMT test -> methylated (OS benefit) vs unmethylated (minimal benefit)

beats.narration_text: Now ask what trials exist for the unmethylated group — the group with no approved good option.

props.command:
claude "What current clinical trials are testing
alternative or intensified regimens specifically
for MGMT-unmethylated GBM? What approach shows
most promise?"

beats.narration_text: Revised tree. Unmethylated node now has a dotted branch: alternative regimen under trial. No Phase 3 evidence for any alternative yet. CCNU plus temozolomide combinations, tumor-treating fields, and checkpoint inhibitors all in trials but no Phase 3 data showing superiority for unmethylated GBM.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: same tree + dotted CRIMSON branch from unmethylated node; trial options listed

beats.narration_text: The counterintuitive lesson generalizes: a broken repair gene helps the drug work because temozolomide damages DNA that a functioning MGMT gene would fix. Silence the fixer, and the damage accumulates. The same logic applies anywhere a DNA repair pathway is the tumor's defense against a DNA-damaging agent.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- summary card: broken repair -> drug works; generalizable principle

beats.narration_text: Verify MGMT methylation status for any GBM patient before temozolomide decisions. It is required by standard molecular pathology and covered by most payers. The test is methylation-specific PCR or pyrosequencing. If unmethylated, the evidence for temozolomide benefit is weak — enroll in a trial.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next steps: verify MGMT before TMZ; enroll unmethylated in trial

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
